concept: Weep / Cry / Let Sadness Flow Out
description: To weep: tears flowing. Completes the sadness family as its outward act: 'nuhe' (sadness, the feeling) → 'nuhemi' (grieve, carrying it) → 'nuhewa' (weep, letting it flow). Tears themselves are 'mirae phialu', eye-water.
sound_symbolism: Begins as all the family begins — 'nu', the grounded deep, 'he', the exhale — and ends in 'wa', outward flow. Sadness leaving by the gentlest door.
grammatical_notes: Intransitive: 'lopia nuhewa.' (The child weeps.) 'mia to nuhewa.' (I wept.) Consoled: 'shia nuhewa. mia wei shia phamo.' (They weep. I console them.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Weeping is grief's completion, not its failure; the family of words is built so the flow has a name of its own.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Tears witnessed without correction are the beginning of consolation; the verb makes them speakable without apology.
